text.title: code-decomposition-principles Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for code-decomposition-principles as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: A single file (often named utils.py or helpers.ts) accumulating 1000+ lines of unrelated functions from across the domain.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No single developer owns it; it becomes a dumping ground; every feature touches it causing conflicts.

failure-mode.description: One function per file: add.py, subtract.py, multiply.py — each 3 lines.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Navigation overhead exceeds the cognitive saving; import chains become deep and fragile.

failure-mode.description: A service class receives an HTTP Request object and accesses request.query_params directly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The service is now coupled to the HTTP framework; testing requires a real or mocked request, not plain data.

failure-mode.description: An index.ts that re-exports everything with export * from './UserService', './OrderService', etc.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wildcard re-exports break tree-shaking; bundlers cannot remove unused exports, inflating bundle size.

failure-mode.description: Mechanical line-count splits that ignore semantic cohesion: user_part1.py / user_part2.py.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Arbitrary splits break logical units; require a responsibility-based target tree before any move.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses cat old > new && rm old instead of git mv.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: git blame and git log --follow break permanently; mandate git mv in the system prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits only the moved file and forgets to update call sites in sibling packages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-file refactors silently break imports; require grep-first + import-update + typecheck before committing.
